text: WOULD NOT RECOMMEND AND I WOULD NEVER RETURN TO ANY FOX RENT A CAR LOCATION. first starters, when I got there the first vehicle they gave me the tire light was on and when I was trying to get out of the parking spot and I pressed the brake, the tires were screeching. I went inside, expressed my concern. They gave me another vehicle I got in that vehicle and when I turned the vehicle on a whole bunch of lights came up on the dashboard as pictured and the vehicle was not heating we waited in the car for 20-30 minutes in the vehicle still wasn’t heating we went inside expressed our concern the gentlemen went out to the vehicle, then came back in and said once we start driving, the vehicle would start heating all the lights will go away, and he explained how his vehicle is the same exact way because we had reservations coming up. We trusted his word and we went on our way now the vehicle did start heating. The lights did go away, but when we were driving, we realized the hood was bouncing. now the contract we signed did say the minute we leave the lot anything that happens to the vehicle is not their responsibility and can’t return the vehicle and what not and I did pay $400 so I decided to proceed with the vehicle because it was driving and we did have reservations going up in the hood. Mind you, the bouncing was not our fault. so we go to the Airbnb we get back in a vehicle and the tire light comes on. I’m thinking the same thing that happened earlier was happening again and we start driving it would go away. It did not go away when we checked the tire pressure the pressure and a tire was 20 psi. I’m thinking OK maybe it’s the temperature that’s affecting the psi in the tire not that they gave me a vehicle with low tire pressure mind you it was snowing. I’m not even from Colorado. I’ve never driven in snow before and I’m thinking no way the rental company would do anything like this, right? OK so we go to our reservation we get back in a vehicle tire pressure light is still on and it never went away. The following morning same thing, psi dropped to like 15 we decided to go to a gas station to put air in the tire which we shouldn’t have to do because I already spent $400 on this rental. I shouldn’t have any issues, let alone pay to put air in the tire we put air in a tire. The air wouldn’t go in the tire so we called out the gas station clerk to help us. It wasn’t working the tire goes all the way flat. We go to a tire shop and tire shop wasn’t open. We called the owner and he was a few minutes away. He came and helped us out but he said because he helped us out before he opened he had to charge us. I was very unhappy about that because I shouldn’t have to pay anything additional for a rental I paid $400 for so I called the rental company. They transferred me to roadside assistance which I’m not sure why roadside assistance would help but anyways, the lady I spoke to from roadside assistance was extremely rude. She was more so worried about the phone call my sister was making in the background instead of listening to my concerns. and eventually, all she told me to do was return the vehicle. There was no solution provided and I still had two days left on the trip. I already spent $400. Nothing about getting my money back or the money I spent to add air in the tire twice. Nothing. When I realize she was no help and I was running late for my excursions. We called the excursion place to see if they could do anything for us in order to rebook and they charged us an extra $120 to rebook. Mind you, the only reason why we were late to the excursion was because the rental that I paid $400 for was giving me tire issues??? We get air in the tire and go to the excursion we come back and tire light comes right back on psi in a tire is 22. I refuse to spend any more money and adding air in that tire so I did not add more air in that tire for the remainder of the trip. We drop off the vehicle and we’re explaining our concerns to the woman that was outside. She didn’t provide us with any solution. She just told us to go inside go inside

When I contacted the location, I was told there were no replacement vehicles available and that I had to drive to the airport to exchange the car. Their customer service claimed that it’s safe to drive even if there’s a low tire pressure issue. This forced me to spend about extra 4 hours and drive 100+ miles in a vehicle that was not in a safe condition.  What was even more frustrating is that instead of taking responsibility, they attempted to shift the blame onto me by referencing their protection policy, even though the issue was clearly a pre-existing defect at pickup. That could only cover the cost I spent on inflating the tire.  Go somewhere else and get a reliable car, don’t rent with Sixt!

text: If I could leave 0 stars I would.  I’ve had the worst experience with Avis.  I never leave reviews but I feel the need to warn others about them so the same thing doesn’t happen to them.  Me and my son flew into Bradley international airport for a college visit at UConn in early April.  We were there for less then 2 days but needed a car so ended up renting a car from Avis.  We took good care of it, didn’t eat or drink anything in it, filled it up with fuel, and returned it spotless, looking the exact same as when we got it.  A couple weeks later I get a letter in the mail from Avis saying they’re charging me $450 for an extra cleaning fee.  I called them and spoke to a customer service rep and asked what the fee was for, and he said for extra cleaning.  I said how, we left it spotless.  He put me on hold for about 5 minutes and came back and said the charges were being removed.  Great!  Or so I thought.  A week later I start receiving calls and getting letters from a collection agency.  Avis had sent it to collections!!! Are you kidding me?!  Now I’m calling collections telling them my story, they’re telling me I need to call Avis.  I call Avis back and the gal said all I would need to do was email Avis and get pictures for proof, so that way they would close the case if there was no proof.  Perfect I though.  They can’t prove it because I know I didn’t do anything wrong.  I email them, and all they say is that the charges can’t be removed.  Can’t provide pictures or even tell me what was wrong with the car.  Part of me wants to pay the $450 just to be done with it and stop wasting time dealing with a terrible company trying to scam me, but I know that’s not the right thing to do. So here I am writing this to warn anyone that’s thinking about using them, Don’t!!!  They might try to scam you, like they are to me!  I am telling everyone that I know about this to help protect them as I don’t want this to happen to others.  This isn’t right and they should be ashamed.
address: 180 Schoephoester Rd, Windsor Locks, CT 06096, USA

text: I travel extensively and don’t hand out praise lightly. What made this experience stand out was Nahnd. I had called the location to ask whether it might be possible to pick up my rental a day early, and she was incredibly accommodating, inviting me to come in that same afternoon. When I arrived, she took the time to walk me through what was available and thoughtfully recommended a Subaru Crosstrek, which turned out to be an excellent choice.  What truly distinguished this experience was her demeanor—warm, genuinely caring, professional, and service-oriented in a way that feels increasingly rare today. She made the process effortless and pleasant, not transactional.  The location itself was easy to find, pickup was seamless, and the return process was equally smooth and welcoming.  After hundreds of rentals over many years, I can honestly say this was one of the most positive rental car experiences I can remember. text: This review is intended to formally document an unresolved and serious issue involving the loss of personal property and inconsistent communication from management. After renting a vehicle from this company, the car was towed while still containing our personal belongings, including high-value electronics. We acknowledge that the vehicle was messy due to family use; however, we did not abandon the vehicle and fully intended to clean it. The condition of a car does not eliminate a rental company’s responsibility to exercise reasonable care over a customer’s personal property or to provide an opportunity for retrieval. When attempting to recover our belongings, we received multiple conflicting explanations from the owner. Statements were made and later denied, timelines shifted, and explanations were offered that did not align with one another. This pattern of inconsistent communication made it extremely difficult to understand what occurred and raised serious concerns. We were also nearly charged $300 for tow reimbursement, despite having a deposit already on file, which was not initially acknowledged during the discussion. This further contributed to concerns regarding accuracy and transparency. Because the electronics remain missing and no clear, consistent account has been provided, a police report has been filed, including a request to pursue charges related to the handling and loss of our property. This step was taken only after repeated attempts to resolve the matter directly. Additional concerns: The tow of the vehicle was not communicated to us at the time it occurred; we became aware only after being contacted with questions about the keys. Photographs taken after the tow appear to show personal items still inside the vehicle, which contradicts later explanations regarding when or where the items went missing. When this discrepancy was raised, we were told follow-up would occur with a third party, but no further communication was provided. This review is not about vehicle cleanliness. It is about duty of care, accurate communication, and accountability. Based on our experience, those standards were not met. Future customers should remove all personal items immediately and document all interactions carefully.
address: 2909 Lancaster Ave, Wilmington, DE 19805, USA

text: I rarely leave negative reviews, but my experience with Budget Car Rental was so disappointing that I feel other customers should know what happened.  I reserved a one-way rental from **Orlando International Airport, Florida** to **Beaumont, Texas**. My reservation clearly showed a pickup time of **9:00 AM on June 25, 2026** and a drop-off time of **9:00 AM on June 26, 2026**.  I arrived about 30 minutes early to pick up the vehicle. The employee at the Orlando counter told me that because I was early, I would have to pay for **two rental days** instead of one. I told him that I was not willing to pay for an extra day and that I was perfectly happy to wait until 9:00 AM, which was the time on my reservation.  The employee then told me he would waive the extra day charge.  What he did **not** tell me was that he changed my contract.  Without my knowledge or consent, the return time on my rental agreement was changed from **June 26 at 9:00 AM** to **June 25 at 6:00 PM**.  Think about that for a moment.  This was a one-way rental from Orlando, Florida to Beaumont, Texas—a drive of approximately **14 hours**. Yet the contract was changed to require me to return the vehicle only about **9½ hours after pickup**, making it physically impossible to comply.  I had no reason to believe the contract had been changed because I had specifically refused any modification to my reservation and had agreed to wait until my scheduled pickup time.  When I arrived at the Beaumont location to return the vehicle, I noticed unexpected charges on my bill and questioned them before paying.  The employee there told me that the additional amount was for **fuel** and assured me that if I paid first, he could reverse the fuel charge afterward.  Relying on what I was told, I completed the payment.  Immediately afterward, the employee changed his explanation and said the charges were **not** for fuel and that he could not issue any refund.  That was extremely frustrating because I would not have authorized the payment had I been given accurate information before my card was charged.  There was another issue involving the toll program.  When I rented the vehicle, I accepted the unlimited toll option after being told it would cost approximately **$12 plus taxes**. Instead, I was ultimately charged **$104.72** for toll-related services. I have yet to receive a satisfactory explanation of how that amount was calculated.  After returning home, I contacted Budget Customer Service hoping this could be resolved professionally.  Unfortunately, my concerns were largely dismissed. Despite providing documentation showing my original reservation and explaining what occurred at both rental locations, I was unable to obtain a meaningful investigation or resolution.  Everyone can make mistakes. What disappointed me most was not necessarily the initial problem—it was the lack of transparency and the refusal to properly investigate what happened.  My advice to anyone renting from Budget:  • Compare your reservation to the rental agreement before leaving the counter. • Verify that your pickup and return dates, times, and locations have not been changed. • Read every charge before paying at return. • If something doesn't seem right, ask for a printed explanation before authorizing payment.  I hope Budget reviews this situation seriously because this experience has significantly reduced my confidence in renting from them again.
address: 1 Jeff Fuqua Blvd, Orlando, FL 32827, USA

text: Had a mixed time using Alamo. We've always used Alamo on our trips to Orlando and this was the first time we've had a bad experience. Tried to exit giving the credit card we used to book and my driving licence, but the person on the stand at the exit said I needed a credit card in my name (we booked using my wife's card - although same surname). Well I didn't have a card on me on my name and for nearly an hour we sat at the barrier whilst no one really knew what was going on. Eventually I had to park the car up and everything had to be rebooked! We had to go back in to the airport building and wasted nearly and hour and a half - after a 9 hour flight, the kids were ratty and tired, this was the last thing I needed! It did get sorted and the people who sorted it were apologetic and offered us a discount plus an upgraded car, but it was stressful nonetheless. This however wasn't the end of our problems.. On about day 3 of our 14 day trip a stone flicked up off the road and chipped the windscreen, later that day the chip spread and cracked across. I rang roadside assistance who asked if I'd randomly told the police (who on earth reports a cracked windscreen to the police) and then suggested I drive an hour to the airport to replace the car - not really what I want to do on my holiday.. they said there is a car care centre near disney and to take it there instead. So off I went and when I got there they didn't have anything except some small sedans. I ended up persisting with the cracked windscreen in the end, but I think the better service would have been for a car to be either sent to us or at the very least the closest alamo to us. As I've said we have used Alamo a few times and maybe this was just the unlucky one.

text: 3.5 stars  Overall, my experience was good, but Hertz could significantly improve the customer experience with better signage and clearer communication from staff  When I arrived, there were signs everywhere directly guests that were a part of their elite membership programs, but I didn't see any clear signage or direction for regular customers. As a result, I walked into the first booth I came across and waited to be helped, unsure if I was even in the right place.  The representative who assisted me was friendly and seemed to recognize that I was in a bit of a hurry, and I appreciated his efforts to move the process along. However, he didn't do a great job explaining what was happening. While I was trying to provide my reservation information, he asked me to step out of the booth and check whether my name was on "the board." I wasn't sure which board he was referring to or why I needed to check it, and no explanation was offered.  Despite the somewhat confusing check-in process, the experience improved considerably from there. I was pleasantly surprised to learn that I could choose my own vehicle from a designated section rather than having one assigned to me. As a customer, I value my autonomy and being able to make the best decision for myself, and I appreciated the opportunity to select a vehicle that suited my preferences. It was a small detail, but one that made a meaningful difference in my overall experience.  I also appreciated that the vehicle was very clean and smelled quite nice when I entered it.  With better signage for non-elite customers and a little more clarity about what is expected during the check-in process, this could easily be a five-star experience. The service itself was acceptable and the vehicle condition was good but it was the lack of direction and communication that created unnecessary confusion.
place_name: Hertz Car Rental - Orlando - Orlando International Airport (MCO)
address: 1 Airport Blvd, Orlando, FL 32812, USA

text: The only reason I am giving a 2 star review is because of the nice young women that helped me out. text: The pickup experience at Sixt ATL was actually smooth. The new key process took very little time. Unfortunately, that’s where the positive experience ended. I rent A LOT and in 2025 I rented cars 256 days due to work so, I feel qualified to leave this review. On average, Sixt gets between 13% to 15% of my  spend primarily related to this branch and DFW consistently not executing (someone caught a stray (: ) . The LAX, JFK, FLL, LAS, PHX, SFO and MSP are usually fine and get the majority of my Sixt spend.  Back to the story, the vehicle itself was not in acceptable condition. First, the fuel tank was only about 3/4 full at pickup, which is not what I expect when renting a car. Second, the exterior of the vehicle was not cleaned and it was visibly dirty, which immediately raised concerns about overall inspection standards.  More importantly, once driving, there were serious mechanical and quality issues. At speeds over 40 mph, the car shook significantly, which made the drive uncomfortable and concerning from a safety standpoint. On top of that, the center rearview mirror was coming apart, clearly showing wear or damage that should have been caught before the vehicle was released. Clearly this car was involved in a major collision and I’m just sharing because, Sixt will be real quick to charge you so document the condition if your rent with them.  In short: great pickup process, but the vehicle condition did not match the level of service I’ll usually would expect at this price point. On the other hand, National Rent-A- Car executes flawlessly every time so maybe they should poach from someone over there. I would consider Sixt again because they seemed to have improved so just proceed with awareness.

text: I've never received a car in this sort of state before. Utterly filthy and damaged. After waiting the better part of an hour for my rental with a toddler in tow. My wife and I loaded up our Chrysler Pacifica in a dark, barely lit garage. Under those conditions the vehicle looked fine, I noticed a little dirt in the trunk but didn't think much of it. The vehicle also had a ticket on the dash that stated it had been cleaned and sanitized.  Once we left the facility and started our 96 mile drive to get to a graduate event on time, we noticed the vehicle was a lot dirtier than I realized. Dried foods and liquids were all over. We were running late and were already a meaningful distance away from Atlanta, we decided we would call customer service once we got to the hotel and see what could be done.  My first call to customer service was apathetic to say the least, they didn't take any of my information and told me I could trade in the vehicle at my nearest facility. There aren't any near me in Auburn. We got cleaning supplies and I spent an hour cleaning and documenting the state of the vehicle. In the sunlight I could see that the car had a dent in one of the doors with a scratch down to the metal. There were pieces of sandwich all throughout the 3rd row, sun block lotion rubbed all over the back of the seats and surfaces, dried grease, food, and drink all over the rear seats. Candy wrappers. scuffs and dirt.  Sticky hand prints all throughout the interior and on exterior surfaces.  The vehicle was in an incredibly poor state. There is also a loud noise coming from the drivetrain at low speeds when maneuvering within parking lots. Who ever had this car before me treated it like utter crap, I just can't treat cars that way.  I tried calling the Atlanta location directly and was again connected to an apathetic remote customer service representative who could barely be bothered to respond to me to the point I thought we were disconnected multiple times, but she was just yawning. They finally sent me an email where I could submit my photos of the damage and the filth. This has been huge pain and changed the nature of what should have been a mix of school and a small vacation with my family. I don't know how a car can be returned like this and then go right back into service without so much as a vacuuming.

text: I had originally rented a premium Dodge Durango (or similar) and received a basic Dodge Durango (cloth seats, no sunroof, no frills).  All options in the lot were basic trims with cloth seats and no sunroof.  But it was clean and comfortable so we went with it and it was a great vehicle for our first 6 days.  After 6 days we decided we wanted to upgrade to something larger as the luggage space just wasn’t enough for an excursion we were going on with 7 passengers on board.  I called several branches and no one had a larger vehicle, and they said we would have to just go back to the airport and hope they have something since you can’t get them on the phone (1 hour round trip).  Luckily there was a beautiful Jeep Wagoneer L which we scooped up.  There was only one other option (Chevy Suburban) which had a weird smell so we went with the Jeep.  My only initial disappointment was that there was no sunroof again, and looks like that is just standard across all vehicles I could see.  The garage was dark and the interior was black so we couldn’t really see the interior until we got home.  Once we got to our destination, we noticed the interior had maybe received a quick vacuum, but the previous drivers had sunscreen stains on the seats, food smeared all over the seats, beach sand in many areas, etc. We didn’t have any time to go back and swap again and our youngest has severe food allergies (peanut, soy, etc), so my wife spent the next 30 minutes cleaning out and wiping down the interior of the vehicle.  We always wipe down vehicles anyways but it’s usually a 5 minute job.  I went in the app to look at options for sending feedback, and it then noticed that I had been back charged as if I had this vehicle since the beginning of our rental (extra $200 when it should have been $80).  I sent all of this feedback through both the app and via email, with pictures (and followed up via email) but have yet to hear from Enterprise.  First message March 2nd, second on March 5th, then shared again when I dropped off the vehicle.  When I returned the vehicle, I shared this feedback and the billing was adjusted for the overcharge, but nothing offered for the condition of the vehicle.  To be fair she asked what she can do and I mentioned that I already sent a message to corporate and would await their response since we were running to our flight.  She said she would adjust the billing and she did right away which was great, and I’m grateful I didn’t have to wait for corporate.  Please keep in mind this is coming from someone who used to work for Enterprise 20+ years ago, at an airport.  I know the standard we had then and I would have wanted this feedback.  I hope to receive a call or have a response that you received the feedback this time.
address: 300 Rodgers Blvd, Honolulu, HI 96819, USA

text: We had a somewhat mixed experience using Car Rentals of Hawaii  Positives: The price is great We had no issues with the car. It had 28,000 miles on it and showed a little wear-and-tear but no complaints  Negatives: Communication is the big issue here.  We had a reservation to pick up the car a little after noon, but with some delays related to the flight and getting an Uber, we get to the office at 1pm to find there is no sign of anyone and spend 10min calling the two numbers they have on the wall. Someone finally calls us to let us know that the car we were supposed to get got broken into the night before.  They tell us they can maybe get a car for us or they can offer us a refund. We opt for the car and they were able to get us one within a couple hours.  We understand that things can happen outside of their control, but the issue I have is that at no point either before our reservation time or in the hour it took us to get to the office did they reach out to us to let us know there was a problem. Instead we arrived at a locked, empty office repeatedly calling and then spent two hours waiting around in the heat without access to a bathroom.  Another minor comment is related to the drop off at the end of the rental period. We were instructed to leave the car in the lot, put the keys in the mail slot, and send an email indicating we returned it. I would've appreciated some kind of acknowledgement from the business that they received the email/car/etc but we just never heard from them again.

text: TL;DR - Our experience sucked.  We had reserved a vehicle for a trip to Hawaii and went to the rental pickup upon arrival. We spoke with an agent (who was rather unfriendly, but that is beside the point). She gave us a Chevy Traverse which seemed suitable for our needs. We loaded up all of our luggage and started up the vehicle to depart. We then realized that it may be a good idea to document the condition of the car prior to departing. Upon inspection, we found that the front right tire had a rather large slit which exposed the interior cords. This is incredibly dangerous and I am concerned that this vehicle was offered to us in the first place in this condition. Compromised tire cords are highly susceptible to blowouts and endangers every passenger. As an established car rental company that has such high-tech cameras that keep track of damage, I am baffled that this was missed. And if not, even more concerned that it was overlooked.  We went to the Gold service center inside of the garage to speak with a different agent about this issue. She confirmed she could exchange the car for us but did not share any concern or remorse about the issue we had discovered. She gave us a Ford Explorer and assured us that this vehicle was larger than our original. We walked out to the car and realized it was actually much smaller and our luggage would not fit. We went back to the Gold center and spoke with another agent (who again was quite rude) and she told us there is nothing that can be done, and if we are still concerned we need to go back inside and speak with our original agent.  At this point we had become frustrated with the entire situation and exhausted by the poor customer service and lack of concern for safety. We went back to our original agent who again switched out our car to a Kia Sorrento which did not resolve anything since our complaint was about the smaller size and the Sorrento was even smaller than the Explorer. We tried to explain the situation and how we already loaded all the luggage into a damaged vehicle but she shared absolutely no care for our situation. Her best response was to upcharge us for a larger vehicle.  Honestly, this feels ridiculous. We were given a vehicle with a damaged (and dangerous tire) after we had already loaded everything up and was met with no concern or assistance. A simple complimentary upgrade would have solved this entire situation and avoided this communication altogether. Is this really the best Hertz can do? I'm also baffled that the customer service was so cold and unfriendly. How can you expect to have a business built upon providing service to customers while having agents that seem to hate their jobs and the folks they service. This experience has left a sour taste for our entire group and we will avoid booking with Hertz in the future.
place_name: Hertz Car Rental - Honolulu - Daniel K Inouye International Airport (HNL)
address: 300 Rodgers Blvd, Honolulu, HI 96819, USA

text: I will never rent from Hertz again. You tell customers to prepay to “save time,” then waste it anyway—while we cook in a concrete garage in paradise.  Get it together. This wasn’t an inconvenience. USAA has a deal with some rental car companies if you have insurance through them that you can rent a vehicle through them EVEN IF you are under 25 or 21 whatever the age is. As long as you're over the age of 18.  I flew all the way from Alaska down to Mobile to go on a vacation since this is my hometown and wanted to revisit after years of being away. I get to the Budget counter for the lady working there to tell me I couldn't rent a vehicle through them since I was underage. I told her the booking was through USAA and that they have a deal with these certain rental companies that people under the age limit CAN rent their vehicles as long as they're a USAA customer. She continued to refuse and then I was stranded at the airport until I had a family friend come pick me up.  I called USAA and got in touch with them the next couple days to reconfirm about this rental issue and how I was allowed to rent these vehicles even though I was 19 at the time. They said yes, called Budget up, and had to remind them the policies. I had also gotten in touch with the manager or some sort of supervisor at this location and he was so nasty and rude to me over the phone and LAUGHED at me about being able to rent with USAA at 19. When USAA got back in contact with them, they said they would apologize to me when I went to go pick up the car.  Did that happen? Nada. Was the manager or supervisor in sight? Nada. Dude was hiding in his office even though he told USAA he would greet me personally and apologize. That's pathetic dude.  I got a rental 3 days LATER then I was supposed to. Got stranded at the airport because of them. And I never recieved an apology.  If you're a military member or military family member with USAA please just avoid them at all costs!

text: I had a truck reserved with Budget Truck Rental for over a month, only to arrive at the location and be told there were no trucks available. The employee casually stated, “Oops, I didn’t call you to inform you,” which is completely unacceptable for a company handling scheduled moving and transportation services.  The experience only got worse from there. I was told there were “no customer restrooms,” despite there being a clearly marked restroom with a sign stating “Customer Restroom.” The lack of professionalism and basic customer courtesy was astonishing.  The employee then suggested I drive over an hour north to another location that “might” have a truck available, but with “no guarantees.” I had her call that location while I was there, and they initially confirmed they had nothing available either. Eventually, they called back and said they did have one truck available — but it was still over an hour out of the way. Because of Budget’s failure to honor a reservation that had been made weeks in advance, I incurred additional rental car charges from returning my other vehicle late, hundreds of dollars in extra fuel costs, several wasted hours of driving, and ultimately had to pay for a hotel for the night. This entire situation created unnecessary stress, delays, and expenses during an already difficult moving process.  To make matters worse, customer service later offered me approximately $40 off as compensation, which honestly felt more insulting than helpful considering the amount of time, money, and inconvenience this caused.  A reservation should actually mean something. This was one of the most disorganized and unprofessional rental experiences I have ever had. I would strongly caution anyone against relying on Budget Truck Rental if your schedule or move actually matters.

text: Even if you are on a Budget, Do not choose Budget!  I prepaid for a Budget rental on May 1, 2026, for the period of May 7–10.  On May 9 at approximately 6:00 PM, the vehicle broke down while my family and I were vacationing near Asheville, North Carolina. We were staying in a cabin in Hot Springs, about an hour away. I spent nearly three hours on the phone with Budget seeking assistance. I was initially told a replacement vehicle was available 15 minutes away, but after multiple transfers and lengthy holds, I was informed that the only option was an airport location approximately 2.5 hours away.  By this point it was around 9:00 PM, causing me to miss a reservation at Jargon, a highly regarded vegan restaurant. Budget expected me to take an Uber 2.5 hours away, pick up another vehicle, and drive 3.5 hours back to my cabin, requiring more than seven hours of travel late on a Saturday night before our scheduled checkout the following morning. Rather than lose the remainder of our vacation, I rented a vehicle from Enterprise, located only 15 minutes away.  After returning home, I contacted Budget. After receiving little assistance, I requested a manager, who initially offered only a one-day refund despite the vehicle failing during the second day of the rental. After considerable discussion, I reluctantly accepted.  A few days later, I discovered that instead of receiving a refund, I had been charged for an additional day. I called multiple times and was repeatedly told that Budget's records showed a refund had already been processed, despite my bank statement showing otherwise. Promises to escalate the matter resulted in no follow-up.  Around May 21, I called again and was told Budget's system was down. I called daily for a week and received the same response. Around May 28, I was again told I had already been refunded and was instructed to email proof from my bank statement. During that call, a manager became rude after I stated I did not wish to end the call until the issue was resolved and eventually hung up on me. Before the call ended, I informed him I intended to report the matter as fraudulent.  While speaking with Chase Bank, Budget finally reversed the erroneous extra charge, but the promised refund still had not been issued. Chase assured me they would assist through the dispute process if necessary.  On June 12, I contacted Budget again and was once more told they would issue only a one-day refund, despite the fact that I effectively had use of the vehicle for only one full day before it became inoperable. As of this writing, I am still waiting for the refund.  At this point, I believe a full refund is justified. The breakdown itself was frustrating, but the weeks of contradictory information, overcharges, lack of follow-up, repeated promises, and countless hours spent explaining the same situation have made this one of the worst customer service experiences I have ever encountered.  Even if you are on a budget, do not choose Budget.
address: 1000 Glenn Hearn Blvd SW Ste 108B, Huntsville, AL 35824, USA

text: I don't write comments on minor dispute but these guys crossed the line, I think. I have a significantly long, brain spinning incidents : when I reserved one way truck from Lewisville, TX to Montgomery, AL.  I reserved truck at about three weeks ahead, I like everything organized and done it on time. My pick up time 04/30/16 11.00AM. I could not reach there because my flight got cancelled due to weather in Dallas. I booked another flight and was gonna reach Dallas at 3.00PM but the truck rental store would close at 3.00PM so I could not pick it up from there. I requested if I could pick up from another store that opens till 4.00PM. Talked with customer service. Inventory manager called me with confirmation and provided me the new pick up location and Tel. I was so happy, and thanked him. I was still in Alabama by this time and I arranged a friend to drive me from Dallas airport to the pick up store. All was well till now.  I called the store just to make sure I and Budget Truck rental are on the same page. Unfortunately, that store did not have a truck. The guy who even works for Budget Truck Rental in that location said, he was not aware of me picking up truck, and he just rented a truck to somebody else 5 minutes before I called. This store is about 45 minutes away from my address, I just was gonna go there (only for that day) because I needed it so bad since I had to get back to work.  Then I called customer service (CS) to notify what Inventory Manager told me is not actually the reality on ground. Now, I was concerned about the trustworthiness of Budget. Even manager lies? I called original location to make sure I still have my reservation and the truck is available when they open store the following day. Low of low!! inventory manager had cancelled my reservation and that truck was already given to somebody else. I was shocked.  After a while, received manager's call. My question to him was why did he cancel my reservation without telling me and why did he give me the false information about the truck at second location? He got pissed off. All he had to say was he is working hard to help me out. I could not believe when he actually hung up on me before answering my question. Before he hung up he told me, I had no choice but to go to second location (45 minutes away) and pick up the truck following morning. I had more questions for him but he hung up :: (1) How can I trust you if there will be a truck next morning when you have already lied to me? (2) I did not reserve three weeks ahead of time to drive 45 minutes just to get the truck. (3) Why did you cancel my reservation without even asking me?  I called CS again after I landed in Dallas. I felt like I was talking with a school bully. Inventory manager was the worst of all on attitude. I really wanted to bring that into somebody's attention how I was treated. So, I asked for supervisor and CS put me on hold fore about 30 minutes and then I had to disconnect the call. I did not think they cared to talk to me resolve the issue. It did not seem they values a customers a bit. Never dealt with companies that bad. I waited for few hours to see just in case they will call me back with solution. I was wrong again. "Of course, it is Budget Truck Rental and what you expect?" my friend screamed on frustration.  I called them back again. I had to end it I was going through the worst nightmare. Now, CS says it like if it was my fault. His Budget created story was I agreed to drive 45 minutes away the following day when I already have reservation in 2 miles distance. He did not even say "sorry". I was surprised. But, I had enough and I did not care if I had to pay more money to other truck rentals. Due to short time constraints Penske did not have any truck. Luckily, Uhaul still had one for me.  Now, when I see Budget it takes me to the bad memory lane, whether it be Budget Rental Car or Budget Rental Truck.

text: Budget Car Rental Fairbanks:  Extremely disappointing and honestly unsafe experience.  We specifically reserved an all-wheel-drive SUV to handle snowy conditions in Alaska. What they did not tell us at pickup was that the vehicle had all-weather tires, not proper snow tires. Also I think one of the wheel slip sensors was having a problem. Every turn was slipping. Those differences mattered and we found out the hard way. We got stuck in the snow twice. The first time, a Good Samaritan helped dig and push us out. The second time, I had to call AAA for a tow truck to save us.  while stuck there serving time, I contacted the rental office and spoke with someone named Rita. While I was explaining the situation, she kept asking repeatedly if the car was damaged (it wasn’t), instead of addressing the actual problem. I mentioned the wheel slippage warning light flashing constantly, which didn’t feel safe at all. She told me to call back in five minutes so she could arrange help, but also made it clear we would have to pay for it. Fine, whatever, we just needed help; it was getting cold and dark. We called back in five minutes… and she had already left for the day. No follow-up, no support. WTH. She just did that so she wouldn’t have to deal with it. Can you believe that?  Thankfully, my own AAA came through and got us out. When I returned the car the next day, I told the staff that a mechanic should check the car because of the constant warning light. The response? Total indifference. No concern whatsoever.  Bottom line: this felt like a bait-and-switch on snow capability, put us in a genuinely unsafe situation, and came with poor customer service from start to finish. If you’re traveling in winter conditions, I strongly recommend using a different rental company for your safety and peace of mind. Zero stars.
address: 6450 Airport Way, Fairbanks, AK 99709, USA

text: I feel like I need to take up the mantle for this place for those of you who are in similar circumstances to mine. I was extremely apprehensive about booking here after reading reviews, so much so that I had even booked another refundable vehicle at a different provider in case this fell through. There were a lot of opportunities for things to go wrong: it was a one-way rental to Anchorage, my arrival time was after midnight, I had booked through a third party instead of direct through Budget. Add to all of that I had never been to Alaska and was very far from home and after 9 hours of traveling would certainly not want to deal with hassle.  That's why I was relieved to see that the entire experience was stunningly easy and straightforward.  Loke was informative and efficient and we were provided essentially a brand new vehicle that was free of defects, blemishes or any evidence of previous wear and tear. text: My 76 year old mom reserved a car from this location. Her flight to Phx was diverted to Las Vegas due to weather. She got in 4 hours later than expected. She was supposed to pick her car up by 5:00pm (when this location closes).  She called the location to tell them what was going on. The manager said if she couldn't make it by 5:00 she'd have to come the next day and that the price would increase by $100 (for one less day of the rental). When my mom said she didn't understand, that she would be there when they open the next morning and that what was happening was out of her control, the manager responded "Well, it's out of our control too".  My mom called back about 30 minutes later and was transferred to customer service. They said that the price should be the same as long as they don't close the reservation. They said if they close the reservation then the pricing will be based on the pricing for that day.  My 76 year old mom called the location as soon as they opened the next morning to ask. The location said that they could not tell her unless she came to the location, but that it was most likely she could get the same price as her reservation. What? They can't tell her for sure and she has to be there in person! So my kind, elderly mom gets an Uber to go to the location (since I couldn't driver her because I had to work that day).  When she arrives the manager tells her the price will be different, $100 more. Make that make sense. You have a reservation, you can pick your car up anytime prior to 5:00pm, your flight gets diverted due to weather, the place closes at 5:00pm and opens at 8:00am the next day. You show up at 8:00am the next day and they want to charge you a $100 more for a reservation you've had for over a month.  So, my 76 year old mom calls Enterprise, they quote her a price $6 more than her original price from this catastrophe of a business, and she takes the Uber to Enterprise and rents the car. For $94 less than the place she was trying to give her business to, but the staff was too dense to know how to run a business.  Be mindful before you decide to rent from this Budget. Know that there are other choices that actually provide service, and are reliable. Both my family and my wife's family live out of state, they will be renting cars out here for the next several years to come out and see their grandkids. I can assure you NONE of them will rent from this Budget, or probably any budget.
address: 1401 N Arizona Ave, Chandler, AZ 85225, USA

text: I have rented from Budget multiple times before, but I have to leave my honest review because this absolutely should not have happened. I understand that things do go wrong sometimes, but I can say with almost 100 percent certainty that Budget does not properly inspect their vehicles before transferring them to another customer.  I picked up a rental car from this location on 3/13/25, and within minutes of driving it, the tire pressure light came on, telling me to add air. I immediately took it to Discount Tire, where they told me the TPMS sensors in the tires were installed incorrectly—the readings were completely backward. One tire read 70 PSI, another 36, another 10, etc. The staff at Discount Tire managed to fill the tires properly and even mentioned that Budget should never have released a vehicle that wasn’t checked.  On top of that, this wasn’t even the car I reserved. I had requested a Toyota Corolla or a similar vehicle, but when I arrived, there were only two cars in the parking lot. One of them was a white Buick Encore, which they gave me. Since I was leaving for California that same day, I had no choice but to take it after they told me that neither this location nor any nearby Budget locations had any other available vehicles.  Halfway to California, yet another warning light came on—this time for the all-wheel drive system. Again, this was something that had clearly not been checked before they gave me the car.  Then the real nightmare began. The next day, while returning to our hotel, the car abruptly shut down in the middle of an intersection. Every warning light popped up on the dashboard, and the car was completely unresponsive—I couldn’t even turn it off. It wouldn’t move, and nothing worked. To make matters worse, we were nearly hit in the intersection due to the sudden system failure.  Thankfully, someone helped push the car into a dark, deserted McDonald’s parking lot. I had my two children with me, one of whom is autistic, and they were both terrified. We were stranded in the middle of nowhere at night, and when I called Budget’s customer service, I could barely understand the representative because he didn’t speak clear English. He just kept repeating that I “couldn’t abandon the vehicle” over and over. They then offered me an Uber voucher to go an hour away to the San Diego airport to pick up another car. There was no way I was going to leave my mother and my children alone in the dark to take an Uber with a stranger to a place I’d never been.  After pushing back, Budget finally agreed to send a tow truck and bring me another car. When the tow truck driver called me, he said the only car available was a Dodge Charger, so I had no choice but to take it. As if things couldn’t get worse, the tow truck itself broke down, and the driver had to wait for help before he could even bring us the car.  I was already unhappy with the Dodge Charger because it was not what I had originally reserved. I needed a fuel-efficient vehicle since we were driving from Arizona to California. So, we had to go to yet another Budget location to exchange it. Finally, we were able to get the car I originally requested—a Toyota Corolla. Even then, the navigation system barely worked with my phone.  Fast forward to this past Thursday when I returned the vehicle. To my surprise, Budget charged my card not once, but three times—for each of the vehicle swaps, which were due to their negligence. On top of that, they charged me a towing fee, which is absolutely insane. They also charged me for the half tank of gas in the car that broke down, even though it completely died and was undriveable.  In total, they charged my card nearly $1,800 when my original rental was only supposed to cost $474 for a few days.  I will NEVER rent from Budget again. I understand that things happen, but the way this entire situation was handled—the lack of accountability, the extreme inconvenience, and the absurd extra charges—is completely unacceptable.  Thanks, Budget, for ruining my entire vacation and ensuring that I will never rent from you again
address: 6650 E Superstition Springs Blvd, Mesa, AZ 85206, USA

text: We had a horrible experience with this rental location and the employee Freddy who worked there.  My wife and I had rented a car from the Mesa Airport location and were given an electric vehicle.  We do not have an electric vehicle and have never used one.  We did not want the vehicle, but the employee said it was a great vehicle and would be easy to charge.  Skeptical, we took the vehicle, a Subaru Solterra. When we got into the vehicle it was not fully charged and a little dirty.  Driving the first day was fine and we found one charger while eating lunch in Gilbert, AZ.  We let it charge for 1 ½ hours and only received 30 additional miles.  We had other plans for the day and thought that we could find other chargers while on our vacation.  There were no other chargers close to any of the areas that we went to, and our hotel did not have one.  The next day we had planned to go to Scottsdale and drover there.  The Presto App showed multiple chargers in the area.  We went to 5 different chargers and each charger was broken or did not work with our vehicle.  Frustrated and having 40 miles left we called Budget Customer service.  We explained the issue we were having and told them that we wanted a different car.  We were told we could take the car back to any Budget location and exchange it.  There was a location in Scottsdale, so he headed there.  When we arrived, there was a sign on the door saying that the employee was out washing a car.  The sign stated that we could call, and the employee would be right over.  We called 3 times without any answer or call back.  Becoming more frustrated, we called Budget customer service again. text: A few days ago, I went online and prepaid to rent a 15-passenger van for our nonprofit youth organization. We have a trip to Salt Lake City coming up. My pickup was scheduled for Monday, July 27, and dropoff Monday, Aug. 3. I paid $869.06.  Last night, looking through my paperwork, I realized that I was limited to 1,050 miles and it was an additional $.17 per mile over that. I had never seen that before as all rentals I have ever seen had unlimited mileage.  I called Budget customer service five times today. I had to fight to get through the terrible AI each time to get to a human, which took lots of time. Each time, I got told they couldn't help me, and I needed to contact the Budget office at the Tucson Airport. The customer service reps kept giving me different phone numbers, including, eventually, one to Avis, their sister company.  When the Avis rep agreed to help me, I explained that Salt Lake was a 750 mile trip each way and that I needed at least 2,000 miles. The man promptly put me on another long hold. When he returned, he said he couldn't help me. This despite me being a repeated Budget customer, and me explaining the situation regarding the trip and the mileage. I told him my group will also be renting another van in SLC. I finally asked for a supervisor. The supervisor, who also put me on a long hold, said he couldn't help me. He claimed the system wouldn't let him overriide the mileage. With that said, I told him I wanted to cancel my reservation. He said I would get a full refund.  I just checked my email, and it says that I get a full refund, minus $50! This is unconsionable! We are a small, youth nonprofit and every penny matters! When he said "full refund" he didn't say minus $50. I will be disputing this matter with my credit card company.  In the meantime, I went to find a rental with another company. Not only did I find a nicer van with unlimited mileage, but it was much closer to me and cost me significantly less (roughly $250) than Budget was going to cost me. Budget is blowing off customers and knows nothing about customer service or contributing to the community. I will no longer be supporting this terrible company!  I highly recommend taking your business elsewhere for a better deal with people who care about people, rather than AI and making a quick buck off the backs of children.

text: I normally don’t leave reviews, but we were really disappointed with our recent experience at this location. After our flight was delayed several hours, we took the shuttle to the rental counter and found a long line with only one counter open. We were assigned a Tesla, and during my usual exterior walk-around I took photos of cosmetic damage. Unfortunately, I didn’t think to check the inside right away. The steering wheel was literally falling apart, and once we saw the car in daylight the next day, the interior was filthy — sticky residue on the glass roof and visible dirt throughout. Some interior paneling in the back seat was also falling off. I was honestly shocked that Budget considered this vehicle acceptable to rent out.  We called Budget and were told we could swap the car at any location, but when we went to a nearby branch, the agent said we had to return to the original airport. We went back the next morning and the swap itself was quick (no one even asked why we were changing cars), and the replacement vehicle was thankfully in much better condition.  However, when we returned the second car at the end of the trip, we were charged a fuel fee for returning the original Tesla at 64% instead of 70% — even though the only reason we brought it back early was because of its condition. We returned the second car at 85%. We’ll be disputing the additional charge.  Overall, this experience was frustrating from start to finish, and we expected much better.

text: I’ll preface this by saying I booked through Costco Travel, and while I know it’s best to book direct with the business you’re using, I’ve also never had issues prior to this with other rental agencies.  Booking the car was great no issues there, but as many others have stated, I did have about a 45 minute wait in the queue once I went to pick up the rental. Every other agency was dead empty of a line, except Budget/Avis, which had no less than a dozen people in line when I got there.  Once it was finally my turn, the agent who got me checked in was fantastic. I do wish I had gotten his name.  The rental wasn’t the issue, the 4Runner I got was perfect for my needs of transporting my grandmothers wheelchair and luggage. Drop off was simple and straight forward, even outside of their open hours of operation.  The problem is upon returning home I noticed my final invoice was $80 more  then my original booking, which I hadn’t ever had happen before. Usually I pay whatever Costco Travel says I’ll pay. Ok, fine. I wasn’t thrilled, but It is what it is. I wasn’t going to stress over it too much. Chalked the $80 up to some fees or taxes that weren’t disclosed. HOWEVER, 3 WEEKS LATER, I was charged an additional $250. No invoice emailed to me, nothing added to my e-receipt, no contact or explanation whatsoever ever.  So I called, and after talking with customer service I was informed it was a cleaning fee for “excessive sand in the back”. Say what? Firstly this is a rental in California, a vacation state…I could understand a fee if the car was covered in sand from a family of 5 tracking it all through the car.  I was the only occupant of the rental until the day I returned it. I went to the beach once, and when I left the beach I took my sandles off, banged the access sand off of then before I tossed them in the cargo area of the 4Runner.  That’s it. I wasn’t building sandcastles in the back. In the last 18 years of car rentals on our family vacations, when we had 3 kids going to the beach every day, using body boards and wet suit and dealing with the inevitable sand that gets in as a result, I have NEVER been charged a cleaning fee.  So my originally booked $285, 6 day car rental, closed out at $613.98.  To anyone else looking to rent a car from this Budget, I highly recommend you stop somewhere right before drop off and detail it yourself, lest you be charged excessive fees.
address: 1659 Airport Blvd, San Jose, CA 95110, USA

text: My experience with Budget Car Rental at Colorado Springs Airport was beyond horrific—it was an absolute disgrace. The customer service was an outright joke, and the lack of professionalism from the staff and the manager was jaw-dropping. They acted as if doing their basic duties was some personal favor, and that attitude only got worse as things progressed. I had requested to pick up the vehicle earlier, between 9-10 AM, only to be told it wouldn’t be ready until noon. The vehicle I received was nothing short of disgusting. An ID was left on the console between the driver and passenger seats, and there were wrappers from cookies and chips stuffed between the seats in the second and third rows. This car had clearly not been cleaned at all in between customers. At best, it looked like they did a lazy, quick vacuum, which didn’t even come close to properly cleaning the vehicle. I know this because I detail cars myself, and there’s no doubt in my mind that all they did was the bare minimum. I didn't feel comfortable addressing it because the manager was already incredibly abusive and unprofessional, and it was painfully obvious he knew the car wasn’t properly cleaned. Yet, somehow, I’m being charged for a full car detail. It’s outrageous! They tack on fees at their “own discretion” because of the lazy job they do. All that extra time didn’t include cleaning the damn car and then, instead of resolving the issue as any competent car rental company would, Budget and its staff decided to go on the offensive, tacking on bogus fees out of spite. Budget/Avis Employees at the Colorado Springs Airport location are careless and have a hostile attitude, this made it clear that they wouldn’t help resolve the issue, and if anything, I was genuinely concerned that they wouldn’t hesitate to leave me stranded—just like they did to the elderly couple. This was especially concerning as I had a family emergency to attend to and couldn’t afford to be stuck dealing with their incompetence. To add insult to injury, when I calmly explained that multiple employees had already provided false information, the manager refused to take responsibility. Instead of trying to understand the customer’s needs, he chose to be blatantly disrespectful to me and my wife, throwing the ID back at us and telling us to take our business elsewhere. His behavior was utterly disgusting, and it escalated to the point where he became loud and aggressively disrespectful to my wife. We had no choice but to record the interaction due to how unhinged he was. Like all typical hypocrites, the moment he realized he was being recorded, he immediately switched to playing the victim, acting as though he had been going above and beyond the entire time. It was a pathetic attempt to cover his own tracks, as he put on a show of false professionalism, trying to manipulate the situation while hoping to provoke me into a response that he could use against me. Fortunately, I remained composed, even though his behavior was a disgrace to Budget’s so-called “customer service” I wasn’t the only one treated like garbage at this location. The elderly couple was in their 60s or 70s being treated just as horribly, if not worse. They were told to get out of the line and walk away just because they were asking questions regarding their rental. The representative walked away to the back room and the elderly were left stranded, and the staff couldn’t have cared less. This location operates with a complete disregard for customer service and has zero accountability for their rude, retaliatory, and utterly disgraceful behavior. Do yourself a favor and avoid this place like the plague unless you want a dirty vehicle, abusive staff, and baseless extra charges. You'll end up paying extra just to clean a car yourself because the employees here do a terrible job. The only thing the manager and employees at this location seem to pride themselves on is rubbing it in your face that they don’t care about your business and telling you to take it elsewhere. ** No space to upload the video.
address: 7770 Milton E Proby Pkwy, Colorado Springs, CO 80916, USA

text: Review of Budget Center – Monday, October 7th  I arrived early at 8:30 AM for a truck reservation as I was moving from Colorado to New Mexico, and I had my dogs with me. The young lady working there told me that dogs weren’t allowed because the owner was allergic. No problem—I understood and could relate to that.  I mentioned I had a reservation for 9:00 AM, but she told me I’d have to wait until exactly 9:00 AM, or I’d be charged for an extra day. I agreed and waited. At 9:00 AM, I went inside and began the reservation process. I provided my driver’s license and the card that had been used to make the reservation.  She informed me she couldn’t accept the card because it was in Jessica’s name. I explained that Jessica had been with me but had to leave for work, and I couldn’t use my own card because it had been compromised in a theft. She continued to talk over me and remained firm, so I offered another card that I hadn’t planned to use, trying to follow their rules.  At that point, I showed my temporary paper driver’s license, as we had just moved back into the country. She said they couldn’t accept the temporary license. So, I went outside—while managing my leashed dogs—to retrieve my passport. When I returned, I placed the passport and card on the counter, but she told me I was being disrespectful and that I needed to hand the items to her directly. Frustrated, I slid the passport and card over the counter, which, admittedly, wasn’t the best approach on my part.  At that point, she refused to continue the transaction, citing my behavior as disrespectful. Let's recap: I complied with the no-dog policy, waited until the exact reservation time, followed their rules on payment and identification, and provided my passport when my license wasn’t accepted. However, I was refused service because I slid the documents over the counter in frustration, and I take responsibility for that.  After this experience, I decided to go to the U-Haul location just around the corner. There, I was greeted by Shayne P. and Madison, who were friendly and helpful. They accepted all my forms of payment, my driver's license, and got me into a truck right away.  I want to thank the Budget representative who I called on Tuesday, October 15th, to assist me after I was charged a no-show fee. She was very helpful and apologetic about the situation. She represented Budget with great customer service.  As for the young lady at the Budget Center, I sincerely hope she finds peace and send her nothing but love and compassion—because we all need it.

text: We rented a car in Denver and upgraded to a 4WD. They gave us a Dodge Ram pickup which I am fine with because I drive a pickup at home. However, this dodge ram didn't have a proper 4WD "high". It simply had 2WD and 4WD LOCK, (which is what you need to pull someone out of the mud but can't really be driven at higher speeds). But realistically, if you are renting a 4WD for a family vacation, it's going to be used on the highway at some point or another especially with vehicles that don't come with winter tires. A pickup truck in 2WD with summer tires is completely useless in the snow.  A couple days later the 4WD failed because the highway use burnt the clutch pack in the front differential and we needed to change the car. However roadside assistance refused to send us a car because we could still use technically move the truck in 2WD. They said the only thing we could do would be to drive to the closest Budget location to change vehicles. The closest location was an hour away but the person on the phone took the time to call (Eagle Airport) and called me back saying, they don't have a pickup but you could change to a Chevrolet suburban or Ford navigator. I decided to drive the hour to Eagle to change the vehicle because the upgrade was appreciated.  When we arrived at budget eagle airport, the woman working at the counter seemed annoyed that roadside assistance had made such promisses, she said she wasn't upgrading and she was giving me another dodge ram. I said no, we're going to have the same problem and it was clear they had plenty of trucks and a literal pile of keys on the board. I would not have wasted half a day of my vacation to fight with the person over vehicles she clearly had. She then she said she was giving us a small KIA at which I also refused because we were 4 plus ski gear and it wasn't going to work. Next she said she would give us a Ford Explorer but when I went to leave with it, discovered the vehicle smelled like cigarette smoke so I also rejected it fearing I would be billed the 450$ penalty that Budget fines it's users for smoking in their vehicles.  She again refused to upgrade despite the long line of beautiful vehicles outside that I saw as a tried that smelly ford explorer. When I came back in, she first said she could not switch the vehicle, claiming the system would not allow it (which I discovered later was a lie). Finally, she gave us a Buick that had damage all down the right side and within a day needed engine oil as a warning flashed on the dash. It also needed washer fluid.  In summary, there are 2 major problems here. First, the base model pickup trucks Budget is using is unfit for the task of passenger 4WD rental. And Second, the woman at the Eagle airport needed to remove her ego from the situation and simply maintain a basic level of company consistency considering the trouble the guest was having.  Thanks.

text: If zero stars were an option, I would select it.  I am a multi-year Fastbreak customer and have rented vehicles across the country without issue. This particular experience was entirely unacceptable and far below any reasonable standard of service.  This was a 10-day, cross-country rental. The vehicle was returned in proper condition, having been maintained carefully throughout the trip. Upon return, I was accused of a “strong marijuana odor”—a claim that is categorically false. We do not smoke in any form. There was no odor, no evidence, and no objective basis for that assertion.  Rather than approaching the situation professionally, staff immediately treated the accusation as fact and attempted to impose a smoking fee without substantiation. The interaction was not handled as an inquiry, but as a presumption of wrongdoing. Quite embarrassing in front of other customers.  In an effort to resolve the situation, we independently cleaned the interior of the vehicle and returned it for reinspection. At that point, we were told we “passed” provided the odor did not return—again, referencing a condition that did not exist to begin with. This inconsistency only further calls into question the legitimacy of the original claim.  To be clear: there was no marijuana, nicotine, or smoke of any kind present in the vehicle at any point during the rental period. The allegation of a “strong odor” was completely unfounded.  Budget corporate ultimately reviewed the matter and removed the $250 cleaning fee. While that outcome was appropriate, it does not excuse the initial conduct, the lack of due diligence, or the manner in which this situation was handled at the local level.  A review of other customer experiences suggests a concerning pattern involving disputed cleaning fees (to include the EXACT accusations of several other customers re: marijuana odors) unsupported claims, and poor customer treatment. That pattern warrants serious consideration.  Going forward, I will choose to rent from airport locations where there is greater oversight and accountability, even at a higher cost.  I would strongly advise others to proceed with caution at this location.
address: 384 Garden of the Gods Rd, Colorado Springs, CO 80907, USA

text: Made reservations for a 5pm Connecticut pickup. They gave us a different size SUV even though we reserved a certain size months in advance. We were stuck with the vehicle since we had to travel early the next morning and other rental locations would be closing soon. The vehicle was stolen overnight,  48 hours after of arriving at our vacation destination. We later found out that the vehicle they gave us has a very high theft rate. The Budget location in Montreal where it was stolen said they know it has a high theft rate and they refuse to carry it in their fleet. The Montreal location then forced another SUV on us even though we wanted a sedan due to another theft concern. We basically spent an entire day of our vacation dealing with the theft matter even though the theft was not due to our negligence. We were so traumatized by the event that we left our vacation early. When we returned the replacement vehicle to Connecticut the clerk was defending the decision to give us a different size SUV saying that I signed the contract. Now Budget wants to collect from my insurance. This will likely result in the loss of a no claim discount and higher rates. The clerk claimed the company would not know that they were giving us a high theft vehicle. Yeah right. They have data on everything. In my opinion, Budget was negligent and they are responsible for their own loss. I also want to add that Budget and likely other rental companies have zero modern anti theft protections. They have not kept up with the car theft criminals who use technology to steal vehicles such as hacking a car remote and driving away in the car in about a minute. Then we wonder why our insurance rates go up. The corporations should look at themselves for the reasons for losses and higher rates.
address: 58 Mill Plain Rd, Danbury, CT 06811, USA

text: You charged me over $600 for one of the worst rentals of all time. And after viewing all of the issues (which I took pictures of), maybe you will agree.  First and foremost, I understand that it was a holiday weekend and rentals were scarce. However, this is NOT an excuse for ANY rental company to offer their customers subpar vehicles.  Here are a list of my issues: 1. The rep at the counter made it a point to tell me to return the car with a full tank, but as soon as I got in, the tank was half full. (See pic, compare mileage)  2. As soon as I got home, the low tire pressure light came on (I live 12 minutes from the airport-see pic). It was the front right tire. Upon further inspection, a literal chunk of the tire was missing. Over the course of the time we had this car, we had to carry a portable tire pump to inflate the tire at the end of every day. So much for convenience.  3. The second day, the check engine light came on. Amazing! (See Pic for reference). The last thing I expect to do with a rental is to worry about it breaking down and putting my safety in danger.  4. Dirty. All surfaces were sticky and car smelled bad (the air coming out of the ac). Also, when putting the back seats into their upright position, a spring flew out towards my face- is this even normal ?  You gave me the worst car on the lot and apparently think it makes sense to send me a bill for this amount.  Your vehicle was untrustworthy, dangerous and should be removed from your fleet to have maintenance performed on it immediately. Please stop putting the lives of your customers at stake due to your carelessness

text: This was one of the most frustrating customer service experiences I’ve had in a long time—and honestly, it’s disappointing because I’ve always chosen Budges over others in the past. After this, never again.  I picked up the vehicle on March 10 and returned it on March 11. Right from the start, the experience was off—the agent at pickup had zero personality and honestly seemed annoyed that I was even there.  About 15–20 minutes after leaving the lot, I noticed a chip in the windshield and immediately called the pickup location to report it. I was trying to do the right thing right away. The employee asked if it was bigger than a golf ball—I told him no—and he casually said to just mention it when I returned the vehicle. So I trusted that advice.  Then about a week later, I get hit with an email saying I’m being charged around $600 to replace the windshield. Completely blindsided. I called customer service to dispute it and explained everything in detail—especially that I had called right after leaving.  Their response? A generic email saying they reviewed their records and the charges are valid “without exception.” No accountability, no willingness to actually look into what happened.  I called again to push back and was told there was no record of my call—basically implying it never happened. So because THEIR employee didn’t document the call, I’m now stuck with the bill? That’s unacceptable.  I did exactly what I was supposed to do—reported the issue immediately—and now I’m being penalized for their failure to document it. text: When it came time to return our rent a car, my girlfriend did not want me going in the building with her because she knew that I would cause a stink and request a free day that we were charged extra for. That being said, I feel like leaving a critical review for a business in today’s Google-centric world is probably worse. Oh well….  When we had originally reserved a car from here, we had specifically requested an economy vehicle. When we went to go pick up the car, we were told that the only vehicle they had in a ECONOMY class vehicle was a Ford Mach EV. For those not familiar with that car, it’s a fully electric vehicle.  The car is absolutely fun to drive. Great torque and safety features. However, it is about as far from an economy vehicle as you could possibly get. To me, an economy vehicle is inexpensive to operate and gets great gas mileage. Unfortunately, we had to drive this car to Tallahassee, which proved to be extremely problematic. It gets about 190 miles on a full charge and a full charge takes about an hour to complete, and that’s if you are able to find a high-speed charger. As a comparison, my Volkswagen Taos gets about 400 miles to the tank full and takes about five minutes to fill up.  So, not only did we have to charge the stupid thing frequently, paying for the charging is actually twice as expensive as filling up a traditional gasoline powered vehicle. Additionally, because we had to charge the thing so often, we were late, bringing the car back and had to be charged an extra day. Not cool Budget!  I think there was a Seinfeld episode involving a rent a car company being great at taking reservations, but not being able to fulfill them. That was definitely the case here. Yes, we agreed to take the electric car. However, it was the ONLY car they had on the lot. So, they were great at taking our reservation and not great at fulfilling it. It smells a little like a bait and switch to me, but that’s up for debate.  Most people don’t have electric cars and are not familiar with what’s required to maintain them. Budget could’ve done a better job of educating us on what to expect with this particular vehicle. They could’ve been more understanding about the charging situation and comped the extra day they charged us. Electric cars are great for cruising around the city if you have a charger at home. They suck as a rental. Don’t let them fool you into taking a fully electric rent a car if you’re here on vacation or planning a road trip. You will be pissed off and disappointed.

text: I am submitting this statement to document my recent experience with this rental car location, which I consider both highly unprofessional and potentially deceptive in nature.  I reserved a vehicle for a four-day rental period at an agreed-upon rate. Upon returning the vehicle, I was presented with a final bill that was two to three times higher than the contracted amount. When I contacted Budget/Avis customer service, they were unable to locate my reservation or provide a receipt, and I was directed back to the branch where I rented the vehicle.  At the branch, I was informed that I had been charged a $450 cleaning fee due to pet hair allegedly left from a prior renter. However, further discussion revealed that the inflated charges were not primarily due to this fee, but rather the result of daily insurance charges and undisclosed add-on fees that were never explained or agreed to at the time of signing the rental agreement.  The branch manager, Issac, was unable to provide clarity regarding my reservation, receipt, or a clear breakdown of charges. His demeanor during the discussion was combative and unhelpful, and he appeared uncertain about the company’s own policies and procedures.  Due to these unresolved concerns, I will be pursuing this matter with my bank, Cherokee County public services, and the Better Business Bureau. The billing practices I encountered appear misleading, and I believe they take advantage of customers in vulnerable situations through undisclosed fees and post-rental charges that materially deviate from the agreed terms.  I strongly urge corporate leadership to investigate this branch and its business practices. As a consumer, I have lost all confidence in this location, and I intend to take all appropriate actions to ensure accountability and prevent others from experiencing similar treatment.
address: 100 Riverwalk Court, Canton, GA 30114, USA
