text: AVOID THIS COMPANY – PAY MORE ELSEWHERE AND SAVE YOUR SANITY If there’s one thing I’ve learned from this experience, it’s that Budget’s corporate structure is not designed to resolve customer disputes or stand behind their equipment. I typically rent from Enterprise, but because they don’t offer one-way box truck rentals, I chose Budget. That decision ended up costing me far more than I "saved." I reserved a 16ft truck and car trailer weeks in advance to move out of state. I made it clear to the staff that I had a strict 6 PM departure deadline because of a 14-hour drive, movers scheduled for 2 PM the next day, and my daughter’s 2nd birthday party that weekend. I expected a seamless process, but what I got instead was an exhausting and costly ordeal.  The Nightmare Timeline: Trailer Hookup Issue: After packing my entire home into the truck, I returned for the trailer hookup only to be told the truck wasn’t equipped with the proper towing control panel, meaning no functioning brakes or blinkers. The only “solutions” offered were to swap trucks (impossible after 4 hours of loading) or call roadside assistance.  Roadside Delays: Roadside arrived 4 hours later, fixed the wiring in 45 minutes, and I didn’t depart until 10 PM—already four hours late. Trailer Strap Failure: When I attempted to load my car, the trailer’s straps were rusted and jammed. Roadside had to come back with WD-40 and a hammer, delaying me another 2 hours. My family (wife, toddler, and dogs) waited anxiously so we could travel together for safety. We finally left at midnight. Blown Tire on the Trailer: Two hours into the trip, a trailer tire exploded due to bent fenders I was never informed about. We were stranded at a truck stop for over 5 hours while roadside sourced a rim and tire. My baby spent nearly 24 hours in a car seat, and we slept in the truck stop parking lot worried for our safety. The Financial Fallout: Lost $200 deposit for movers Paid an additional $600 for expedited movers Extra food and gas expenses Nearly $2,300 paid to Budget for equipment that was unsafe and poorly maintained  Customer Service Disaster: When I called Budget the next day, the local manager admitted he couldn’t do anything and told me to contact corporate. Corporate initially said they’d “comp the rental,” but then changed their position and only offered $200. After escalating, “Level 2” customer service offered $500 max—but only after I returned the truck, effectively leaving me no choice. It’s been nearly a month since then, and no one has followed up despite their promises.  Final Verdict: Budget is cheap for a reason. The money you save upfront will cost you far more in delays, stress, and out-of-pocket expenses. Their equipment is poorly maintained, their support is painfully slow, and their customer service is structured to deflect responsibility instead of fixing problems.  I have attached pictures just for visualization of all of the issues encountered. Do yourself a favor: rent from a company that values its customers and stands behind its service. I will never rent from Budget again—and if you value your time, money, and sanity, you shouldn’t either.
address: 975 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

text: I recommend taking a few extra minutes to inspect any rental vehicle before leaving the lot, especially checking the safety inspection date and basic fluid levels.  I recently rented a 2022 Chrysler Pacifica for a week. After getting the vehicle home, I noticed the safety inspection had expired, the maintenance reminder indicated the oil change was overdue, and the second-row floor storage compartments could have used a quick cleaning.  When the main customer service line was unable to provide timely assistance, I returned to exchange the vehicle rather than risk potential issues while driving. To their credit, the staff quickly provided a replacement vehicle. While waiting, I checked several other minivans nearby and noticed multiple vehicles also appeared to have expired safety inspections. My concern wasn’t to be critical, but to hopefully prevent another customer from unknowingly driving away with the same issue.  The replacement vehicle had a valid safety inspection, but I noticed the engine oil, coolant, and right rear tire pressure were low. I asked if the fluids and tire pressure could be checked. The staff promptly brought the vehicle to maintenance, though afterward it appeared the coolant level still had not been addressed. At that point, the team again offered to exchange the vehicle, but I decided to keep it since the major safety and registration concerns had been resolved.  I do think the maintenance oversight team could improve its inspection process. Basic items like current safety inspections, fluid levels, and tire pressure should ideally be addressed before vehicles are rented out. Attention to these details helps prevent breakdowns and gives customers more confidence in the condition of the fleet.  That said, I want to recognize the customer-facing staff for being respectful, patient, and willing to help throughout the process. In particular, the on-duty manager, Peter, was professional, attentive, and genuinely listened to my concerns. He handled the situation very well, and I appreciated his attitude and effort.  For other customers: regardless of which rental company you use, it’s always worth doing a quick inspection and documenting any issues before leaving the lot. A few extra minutes upfront can save time and frustration later.
address: 300 Rodgers Blvd, Honolulu, HI 96819, USA

text: The service at the desk is the worst compared to any other USA locations due to inefficient desk agent, coordination issues and poor direction. I’m an avid traveler and had been a budget fast break member for several years. I never had this kind of inconvenience. I understand this location operates inside a mall but when fast break members have advanced reservations, it is expected that the wait time would be minimal to zero. The option to pick keys (self service) or walk directly to car area should be available no matter what. At the very least, there needs to be a pick up brochure or an agent to help the members w/out line wait. The directions to the desk location were nowhere and there’s no signage inside the mall. Most shop vendors (on the mall first floor) also had no clue of budget desk location. Google Maps vaguely mentions first floor. To add to this incompetence, the agent at the desk was grossly slow and incompetent. People had to wait for over 20 minutes. Also, we were not able to find our car on the fifth floor as instructed and we had to go back down to wait another 20 mins to get another reassignment in the same vehicle class. Turns out, their work crew may have wrongly parked the car on 6th floor instead of 5th. The first agent at the desk didn’t know how to track this information whatsoever. Our itinerary had been badly affected due to the delays and inefficiencies. Avoid this location in future if possible and will definitely trust the recent google reviews.  Future tourists: The desk location towards the end of first floor escalators and is on the food court side (all the way near target). For drop off during off hours, enter through the parking garage entrance for the market place and head to the level 5. Make sure you return the 5 things (rental drop off date, drop time, mileage, fuel range and parking ticket) as in the attached screenshot. The drop box is located near the elevators.
address: Oahu Island, 2330 Kalākaua Ave, Honolulu, HI 96815, USA

text: Theft and lies by Budget and contractors.  Returned car to hotel valet Tues afternoon. Mistakenly left Bose headphones in center console.  Checked with valet early Wed morning.  Car still there but no headphones... valet said car was already cleaned.  Then checked with Budget employee once counter opened later in morning... no headphones.  Budget employee said will check with 3rd party cleaning service at hotel and will call me back.  2 days later on Fri afternoon, still no return call, so came back to counter with different Budget employee who said cleaning service is at international marketplace or airport (not hotel as first employee mentioned).  Informed me that the lost and found person at airport is not available on the weekend, so will have to wait till Monday.  Here's the problem:  Lack of accountability by Budget and fragmented chain of custody among hotel staff, third party contractors and Budget employees across multiple locations (Waikiki hotel, International Marketplace and HNL airport).  Spoke with 2 different Budget employees who conveyed 2 different stories on how and where vehicles are cleaned.  So, if my car was still at the hotel when cleaned, the headphones should still be there.  However, the second Budget employee indicated that the lost and found could be elsewhere.  So, which employee is correct and who is lying?  The bottom line is that Budget doesn't care.  I had booked other daily rentals from Budget but decided to let them lapse due to their lack of care and accountability.

text: I’m wondering how many customers have overpurchased fuel to cover the difference of their tank not being full when they pick up their rental vehicle?  I picked up my rental KIA vehicle from the Hilo Airport location at 7am in the morning. The mileage was 51833 miles and the fuel gauge indicated a full tank, as it should be when picking up a vehicle. After attending a funeral service and completing some short errands, I filled the tank at 11:20am at a nearby gas station. I filled the tank with 2.895 gallons of gas @ $5.699.  It seemed like a lot but I needed to fill the tank to full. When I returned the vehicle to the Budget Hilo airport at approximately 11:30am, the odometer read 51854.  I realized I only drove the rental car 21 miles. It didn't seem right that I refilled the tank with nearly 3 gallons of gas and only drove 21 miles. That would have meant that the KIA vehicle only got 7 miles per gallon?  At the airport I went up to the Budget counter person and said that I have a question about the refueling of the tank and that it does not seem correct. After briefly explaining the situation, she immediately raised both arms and said she was not the person to talk to, the male coworker next to her added that they don't know if I was sitting in the car for a long time with the engine on and using the fuel!  They never showed any interest and did not care about my inquiry. Adding insult to my situation, I felt they falsely accused me of fabricating my story. Having just attended a family funeral, this is the last thing I needed to end my trip to Hilo!  I've been a loyal Fastbreak customer using my corporate number for many years. I have always rented from Budget and have never been treated this way. I was treated very badly on this day and will not again rent from Budget Hilo airport, as there are other companies that surely want my business.
address: 2355 Kekuanaoa St, Hilo, HI 96720, USA

text: We have always rented from Budget on other islands on our trips to Hawaii with no problem, but this location has soured our experience and we will never rent from Budget again. When we arrived my husband went straight to pick up our rental car while I stayed with the kids to wait for our luggage. When my husband came back to pick us up at the airport with our rental he told me he had signed up for advance fuel purchase when he picked up the car. I immediately noticed the gas tank was only a third full. I told him he needs to take a photo of the dashboard asap because I have never been given a rental car that didn’t have a full tank of gas in it before. Fast forward 2 weeks later when we went to return the car I made sure to tell them that we are returning the car with more gas in it (2/3 of a tank) than when we picked it up. However, when we received the bill afterwards I immediately noticed we had a fuel charge of $95.06 added onto the rental cost. We were charged for a full tank of gas! Did they even look at the gas gauge at all? I called them. They told me I need to email them with photos of the dashboard showing how much gas was in it at pick up and drop off, as well as receipts for any time we filled up at the gas station. After emailing the photos I received a response a couple of weeks later asking for the photos again. After emailing them the same photos a second time I never heard back from them again. It’s now been over a month since we returned from our trip and we never heard back from Budget. I’m sure they are hoping we had forgotten at this point and never intended on refunding us. Don’t be fooled by the seemingly lowest price at budget. They scam you for more money in other ways. I would give them 0 stars if possible.
address: 3285 Hoolimalima Pl, Lihue, HI 96766, USA

text: ⭐☆☆☆☆  This was honestly one of the most frustrating and disappointing rental car experiences we have had.  After getting off a seven-hour flight, we were exhausted and just wanted to get our luggage, pick up the car, and finally get settled. Instead, we spent about 15 minutes waiting in line just to receive the paperwork, and then another hour and a half waiting for the actual vehicle. After traveling all day, that kind of delay feels completely unacceptable.  The shuttle bus to the Budget location was very crowded and did not have much room, but considering how busy the airport was, that part was understandable and honestly not what upset us. The real issue was the rental car service itself.  When the car finally arrived, it was very disappointing. The vehicle had over 50,000 miles, multiple dents on the exterior, stains throughout the interior, and looked like it had not been maintained very well at all. They did wash the outside before bringing it to us, and the employee helping us was polite, which we appreciated, but it still did not change the condition of the car.  The car also did not come with a full tank of gas, which added even more frustration. On top of that, we had specifically requested a larger vehicle, but instead we were given a very small car with no real accommodation offered.  At that point, after such a long day of travel, it just felt defeating. We expected much better service and much better vehicle quality from a company like Budget. Instead, the experience left us exhausted, frustrated, and genuinely disappointed. Based on this experience, we would not recommend Budget as a company.
address: 3285 Hoolimalima Pl, Lihue, HI 96766, USA

text: My experience with Budget Rental Car was unbelievably frustrating.  After arriving, I was met with a line that wound out the door and into the hot sun. The wait to reach the counter was over four hours. During that entire time, there was no communication from management about the cause of the delay, no updates on expected wait times, and no effort to keep customers informed.  What made the situation even worse was the complete lack of customer service once we finally reached the counter. There was no apology for the excessive delay, no offer of a reduced rate, and no complimentary upgrade to acknowledge the inconvenience. It felt as though a four-hour wait was being treated as normal and acceptable.  The only creatures that seemed satisfied with the experience were the chickens roaming around the property and occasionally wandering inside the rental office eating the hungry patrons crumbs from the vending machine snacks.  If Budget expects customers to endure a four-hour wait, the least they could do is communicate, apologize, and make some effort to address the inconvenience. Unfortunately, none of that happened. text: This location is providing customers with unsafe cars, I am posting a review on behalf of my family who recently rented from this Budget. text: Long story short, I was visiting Yellowstone and had car problems and thought that budget was going to save the day. I reserved a car on Expedia and got a ride over there only to find that they would not rent to me because they require a credit card and will not accept a debit card or a deposit. They happily took the money for the reservation but the instant the receptionist saw that it was a debit card he acted like I was a homeless person asking to use his personal restroom. I have more than enough money in the bank to buy a brand new vehicle so for a receptionist to look down his nose because I don't believe in the usery of credit cards was pretty annoying especially after having already paid for the rental. . . . Luckily the very next kiosk over was Avis rentals and they not only happily rented to me using my debit card, but the clerk there was super friendly and put me into a 4x4 four-door Dodge ram 1500 which was amazing for going around Yellowstone. The best part was that it was $200 cheaper than budget was going to charge me for some little soccer mom station wagon. I drove the truck like it was my own and brought it back full of gas and in perfect shape. If you are reading this, and you are considering budget, I highly recommend that you at least reach out to Avis because in my experience it saved me money and actually prevented a lot of frustration. No stars for Budget rental, they're obviously charging more than they need to, and they are obviously only interested in their own money which is fine for a business so long as they understand the value of customer service which Budget rentals does not. Any place that is popular you are going to find greedy sharks price gouging and committing highway robbery so beware of these kinds of companies.

text: Admittedly, I was quite concerned walking thru that airport towards the shuttle that would bring me to the rental car center as I read the reviews. Car was pristine, drove great and our only hutch was we didn't immediately know how to leave the garage (they text you a scannable QR code, we missed that). Allow about 30mins to walk/shuttle from your gate to the desk, and vice versa on the return. Wish I got the name of the agent who helped, too. She was efficient and friendly (10a on Sun 5/2/26), and seemed to be there on our return as well, assisting another customer (11a on Tues 5/5). She answered all of the questions we asked AS she worked on processing our car, so quick. No issues. Wouldn't normally review this sort of place but given the prior reviews were so alarming, I'm suspecting they took note and made the proper corrections or brought in/trained better staff. text: Down right disgusting, unorganized, terrible customer service. Paid for a car seat and feel an obligation to warn all others, DO NOT RENT HERE, let alone car seats.  Back story: Our son became ill at last minute so we had to readjust everything. Pediatrician approved travel in a week so we changed plans accordingly. Since it was surrounding Christmas, our options became severely limited with only certain Budgets showing vehicles in stock. We changed our flight to go to Midway since no vehicles were in stock at O’Hare. We signed up for fast break (never an issue anywhere else) and our name wasn’t listed nor ready in the section it should have been. When we get to the counter, which is slammed, we heard the employees rudely tell customers the system was showing vehicle available when they weren’t. The woman at the counter was able to get us an SUV, though we had to wait for her to finish a side conversation though we’d been waiting over 45 minutes. She kept waving her hands around speaking to a gentleman regarding bringing the cars up front. We asked politely if those were the keys, she ignored us, proceeded the side convo for another 5 minutes before hammering away on the keyboard then she suddenly finally gave us the keys after another 5 minutes of awkwardly ignoring us. We asked about the car seat and she pointed out the window and said grab whatever available, if he doesn’t fit there’s some in a cabinet. When going to the area, it’s obvious none of these seats have been cleaned and they were sitting in below zero degree weather and cold to the touch, the “ones on the side” were in pretty bad condition as well, with some wet frozen chairs sitting on a miscellaneous bench. It was bad, to make matters worse they had the car pulled up and it wasn’t even cleaned whereas the rest of the vehicles were visibly clean with no salt on them.  By the way, this is the kicker, I had to come back to update this-upon returning my car I tried to use the family bathroom to change my son’s diaper but someone was in there and I could hear speaking so I tried waiting. After ten minutes we figured we’d just use the changing tables in the rest room since nobody was around anyway (I don’t like changing my son out in the open and prefer family). While waiting for my husband a woman finally came out talking away on her phone. There wasn’t a family, a companion or anything, she was an employee complaining about being at work and started pacing the halls. There are two large bathrooms to use, and this employee was using the family bathroom to speak to someone on phone.  I understand it’s busy during the holiday season but these employees do the least possible with no regard to customers on the receiving end. Budget needs to work on their faulty systems, stay away from having to spend $100 on the seats (rounding up) though I covered the seat my son later developed a rash.  STAY AWAY. I’d give zero stars if I could. Pictures for the terrible conditions and forewarning for others regarding the car seats.

text: If I could rate this Budget rental location 0/5 I would. I booked with them via Priceline. I was given a different vehicle than requested which is not the end of the world. However, not only was I given an unclean vehicle that smelled like weed and cigarette smoke. There was a cigarette burn in the seat that was blamed on me. You would think that when you rent from these companies you would receive a well-kept and clean vehicle but that is not the case.  Despite telling the clerk at the front desk before and after, I was assured that I would not be charged due to having photo and video evidence. No one looked at the vehicle prior to be leaving.  I was told that the pre-authorization fee would drop off. Additionally, I let her know that someone had parked a different vehicle in my original return spot which was I was old told was fine.  I took pictures and videos to ensure I wouldn't be blamed for this, which all are time-stamped.  After noticing the charges were not dropped. I phoned customer service to inquire about what was going on. To which I was directed to the billing department. Upon calling them 7 times, I was never put through due the user being "busy" . After that I phoned the customer service line again. I explained what was happening to the agent, to which I was then rudely hung up on. How frustrating. I called back immediately. This agent was more helpful. I was prompted to email my photo and video evidence which was time stamped.  To which I was I got a response that stated, " If the car was like this before the vehicle would be un-rentable". If that is the case, then how was I given the vehicle in such conditions in the first place. I've never smoked a cigarette or weed a day in my life and to be charged over $600cad for a rental vehicle in poor conditions when the car was "un- rentable" is absurd.  I would highly recommend taking your rental car needs somewhere else. As the clerks, customer service and overall company do not care about the wellbeing of their customers.

text: Nothing but problems at the Shorewood location. Communication was horrible. Customer service was even worse. Reserved a Chevy Traverse months in advance. I do not want to get started on the miscommunications regarding credit cards vs. debit cards with this location but I will just say my reservation was at 0900 and I was not able to drive off the lot with the vehicle until 5pm. Vehicle had a check engine light come on before even leaving the lot. Manager and service said the car was fine to drive since they did not have any other vehicles available in the same class and it was closing time. Traded the vehicle in when I arrived at my destination but only after nothing but problems on the drive because of the check engine light. Vehicle acceleration was minimal, vehicle did not want to start when it was turned off, and the electrical system started acting up. I know I should not have driven off with the CEL on but I was in a scheduling crunch and had to get on the road for an opening ceremony. Once I traded it in, everything was fine from then on out. Then comes the return to the original Shorewood location. The customer service phone line and the other rental location told me to speak to the original locations manager to receive credits for the days that the vehicle was basically inoperable. I did that but was told that there was nothing he could do and that customer service could supply credits towards my next rental. I never plan on renting with Avis/Budget again so that does not work for me. Plus they charged me for a less than full gas tank when I returned the vehicle. I am lucky I was able to get the car to the new location without getting stranded, let alone stop at a gas station. An offer to come and pick the vehicle up and swap it was never offered and I do not feel it was my duty to suggest it. The original location manager was able to waive the gas fee so that’s something I guess, but no other compensations or suggestions were offered other than call the customer service line. At the end of the trip, I returned the new vehicle two days earlier than planned and only 75 dollars was credited for those two days when the rate was 104 per day. Make that make sense. I am putting this experience behind me because overall the trip was a success (my son won his baseball championship game) but Budget has lost a customer, not because of the vehicle issues, all vehicles have problems at some point, but squarely based off of the poor customer service and unempathetic attitude from Management at the Shorewood location. I understand that everyone has different experiences but if you choose this location, I hope your experience is nothing like mine.
address: 300 Gregory Ct, Shorewood, IL 60404, USA

text: I’ve lived in Chicago for 10 years and have never owned a car, so I rent cars pretty frequently. Budget on North Broadway is the worst car rental company in the area. I rented here for both Thanksgiving and Christmas 2025 and booked months in advance.  For my Thanksgiving reservation, my car was delayed by 3 hours, so I missed my Thanksgiving plans, and the man who works there has absolutely no empathy and simply says “yeah, you and 100 other people.”  Now, fast forward to Christmas, it is Christmas Eve as I write this… I was scheduled to pick up my car at noon. They called to say it would be delayed two hours but they’d call me at 1:00 with an update. We never received an update, and they close at 2:00, so we called at 1:50 to check in. He proceeds to tell us he’s not going to have a car for us. We have to either go out to O’Hare to get a car or he has to cancel the reservation, and all he’s willing to reimburse is the cost of our Uber. Mind you, it’s an hour drive to O’Hare and adds an hour to my drive to Indianapolis. So again, here I am missing my Christmas Eve plans because they don’t know how to manage their operation, nor can they manage to update me in a timely manner or compensate me for their mistake.  Most people who rent cars do so for specific plans, so if you have to be somewhere in a timely manner, don’t rent from here. When I had a similar experience at Enterprise, they simply drove me to a nearby lot. Budget doesn’t even have a nearby lot to pull from. They drive cars from Indiana so any delay is going to add hours to your trip.

text: A Case Study in Corporate Negligence and Systematic Operational Failure.  As a frequent traveler who manages a rigorous weekly flight schedule and thousands of successful rentals, I am accustomed to the logistical variables of the industry. However, my recent experience with Budget Romeoville (Lewis University Airport) was not merely a service lapse—it was a comprehensive study in corporate incompetence and a blatant disregard for vehicular safety.  The Safety Violation: After paying a premium for a last-minute economy vehicle, I suffered a tire blowout on the second day of my trip. The attending recovery professional—a third-party lifetime towing business owner—confirmed that the tire was in a state of advanced degradation and should never have passed a basic fleet safety inspection.  The Call Center Quagmire:  The subsequent attempt to rectify this via their centralized support was an exercise in futility. I encountered a profound linguistic barrier and a scripted, rigid bureaucracy that was incapable of processing a simple request: a vehicle exchange to mitigate liability and ensure road safety. The "supervisors" were not only unhelpful but actively antagonistic, demonstrating a level of unprofessionalism that borders on the surreal.  The Lafayette Contrast:  After being told a replacement was impossible or would cost an additional $190 (a transparent attempt at price gouging a stranded customer), I bypassed the corporate apparatus entirely. I found a local branch in Lafayette, Indiana, where the staff confirmed the systemic failure of the call center. At the branch they've given up trying to work problems out through the various help numbers. The Lafayette team was super-competent, UNDERSTOOD my simple problem in a few minutes and sent me on my way!  Avoid at all costsThe Romeoville!  Conclusion:  Upon returning to the Romeoville location, the management displayed a chilling level of apathy. Rather than offering a formal apology for the four hours of lost productivity and two missed professional appointments, the manager attempted to "generously" waive a two-hour late fee—a fee incurred specifically because of their own equipment failure!  Final Verdict: Budget’s operational model relies on nickel-and-diming customers while outsourcing accountability to a disconnected global call center. If you value your time, your professional reputation, and your physical safety, stick with Enterprise! This location is a liability. I may share transcripts of these interactions with my social media audience to ensure this level of negligence is properly documented.
address: 1 Hanger Rd, Romeoville, IL 60446, USA

text: When I rent a car, at the very least, I expect it to work and not have any outstanding issues. Of course, I know the car will be used and will not be perfect, but I don't expect a "Warning" message to pop up the moment I drive out of the lot. I rented a Nissan Rogue from this location. The initial rental process was fine, not good, not bad, just fine, maybe a bit slow. Still, nothing to write a review about. But, as soon as I turned right out of the lot, a "Warning" message popped up on the dashboard screen. Of course, this was alarming, so I pulled over as soon as I could and looked for an owners manual to see how serious this was. Of course, there wasn't one. Since I was only a few blocks from the location, I turned around and decided I would either ask them to fix the car or to exchange it for a different rental. When I returned, I let the woman working know that there was a weird "Warning" message on the dashboard and that I was worried about my safety since I had no idea why this was there nor do I think it is my job as the client to troubleshoot why this message is there. Her reply, "yeah, it does that" as she chuckled. What? It does what? Doesn't work? She went on to say if it doesn't turn off then to come back and get another one. To which I say, why don't I just get another one now? Her response: we don't have any more SUVs. Fine. So I drive it back to where I was staying, park, and call Budget. WHAT A WASTE OF MY TIME!!!! I was on the phone with someone for 30 minutes whose only tactic was to continuously parrot the phrase, "please let me know how I can help you resolve this issue" but did ABSOLUTELY NOTHING to resolve the issue. I told this person that the car I had been given had a "Warning" message and that I didn't feel safe driving it. His advice, for me to call around local companies to track down a different car. To which I say, aren't you the company? Didn't I just call corporate Budget? Can't you track down a car for me? His response, well, you have to call the locations yourself to track down your car, and, oh, "please let me know how I can help you resolve this issue. 'Garbage! Do better! I called around to many locations and finally got immediate help at the Avis/Budget location on Rt 59 in Naperville. The woman working there was incredibly efficient and immediately worked to solve any issues that came up. I traded in the "Warning" car with her and drove away with a much nicer, but more importantly, safer, vehicle. But, I lost 3 hrs of time sorting out something that should have never been an issue. BUDGET: YOUR JOB IS TO MAKE SURE YOUR CARS ARE SAFE AND FUNCTIONAL WHEN YOU RENT THEM. IT IS YOUR ONLY JOB SO DO YOUR JOB. Bottom line: avoid Budget if you can, if you can't, avoid this location and save yourself the frustration. If it wasn't for the woman at the Rt 59 location, I would have been stuck with junk.
address: 1115 E Ogden Ave, Naperville, IL 60563, USA

text: DECEITFUL ADVERTISING. I was charged an additional $100 for a vehicle I reserved as I reserved an economy vehicle, which according to Budget's website is a Hyundai Venue or similar (AKA a compact SUV). I was told, both when I picked up the vehicle and returned the vehicle, despite my confirmation email/reservation, that I did not reserve a compact SUV and actually the economy vehicle is the smallest vehicle they have and that all of their smallest vehicles are all electric which I also was not told ahead of time. I was charged the additional fees to get the vehicle I actually reserved and told that if I have a problem with the contract, I would have to contact customer service. Customer service says that I was not charged a fee for upgrading and that the additional charges were for taxes and returning the car to a different location from where I picked it up. I specified in the reservation I was returning it elsewhere, and when I picked up the car there was no mention of this additional fee for returning it to a different location. I have never heard of a rental company adding such a fee after the rental is complete. My confirmed reservation said all fees would be $100 lower than what I ended up being charged. So, I'm out the $100 apparently. But hopefully I can save people from using Budget and their misleading services. I know I will NEVER use them again.

text: I recently had an incredibly disappointing experience with Budget Rental Car on W. Washington Street. When I arrived to pick up my van, they informed me that none were available, and they had to get one from the airport. What I received was nothing short of disgusting—an unclean vehicle that was clearly not ready for rental. There was mud on the step boards and pink residue all on the windows and inside the car, making it even more unacceptable. It was trash in the car ands definitely not vacuumed.  I reached out to the Regional Manager  to express my concerns, but the response was lackluster at best. I was basically told they would just clean the van, which felt dismissive. Given the circumstances of a family emergency that required us to travel, my husband had to spend extra time cleaning the van before we could even use it.  To add insult to injury, they offered me a mere $50 credit for this inconvenience, which felt completely inadequate. It's clear to me why this company is struggling, and after this experience, I will not be renting from Budget again. Next time, I’ll happily pay more for the peace of mind that comes with renting from Enterprise, where I know I’ll get a clean vehicle.
